reasoning_model_1: The model failed significantly in Content Accuracy. Instead of reading the script strictly ('The game is about to start, is everyone ready to enter?'), it paraphrased it to 'The game's about to start, everyone ready to enter?' (00:00-00:03). More critically, the model hallucinated and vocalized internal instructions or style descriptions at the end: 'Fast, sharp, no pause, just go' (00:03-00:06). While the pacing of the initial sentence was arguably fast, the inclusion of non-scripted text and the modification of the target sentence represents a complete failure to follow the core generation command.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the command perfectly. The Content Accuracy is 100%, reading exactly 'The game is about to start, is everyone ready to enter?' without omissions or additions. Regarding the feature 'Pace', the model successfully increased the speaking rate (00:00-00:02), delivering the entire sentence in about 2 seconds while maintaining clarity and articulation. The urgency implied by a fast pace was conveyed naturally.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental constraint of Content Accuracy by altering the target sentence and vocalizing meta-instructions ('Fast, sharp, no pause...'). This severe hallucination warrants a penalty score of 0 according to the evaluation criteria ('Voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script'). Model 2 successfully synthesized the correct text and effectively applied the 'fast pace' parameter, resulting in a natural, urgent delivery that met all user requirements.
